reasoning: The document suffers from significant content loss and structural corruption, most notably the omission of ten procedural steps on Page 4 and critical instructional text like URLs and button labels throughout. These defects are corroborated by a low shingling recall (0.818) and a very poor structural fidelity score (0.45), making the document unfit for automated publication despite the pipeline's heuristic suggestion.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: There is significant text omission in the instructional steps on Pages 1 and 2. In the 'Email', 'Text Message', and 'Phone Call' sections on Page 1, the login URL (https://myncid.nc.gov/) and nearly all button labels (e.g., ADD ENROLLMENT, ADD, NEXT, SUBMIT) are consistently omitted from the text content of the list items. On Page 2, the 'Authenticator' section's second step similarly omits the login URL. These omissions account for more than 25% of the content in these specific instructional li
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 - Email Section: 1. Login to https://myncid.nc.gov/... 3. Click on: [ADD ENROLLMENT] 4. Select 'Email' and click: [ADD] 5. Verify the email address that pops up is correct and click: [NEXT] 6. Enter the 8 digit code that you received in your email and click: [SUBMIT]
concerns.output_quote: "text": "Login to " ... "text": "Click on: " ... "text": "Select “Email” and click: " ... "text": "Verify the email address that pops up is correct and click: " ... "text": "Enter the 8 digit code that you received in your email and click: "

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is mostly preserved on pages 1-3, correctly moving from H1 to H2 to H3. However, on page 4, the sub-headings 'For an existing MFA method' and 'For a new MFA method' (which are visually equivalent to the H3 headings on page 1) are converted into list items within an unordered list (reading_order 60) directly under the H2. This eliminates the subsection hierarchy for that page and represents a major structural corruption.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Visual sub-headings: 'For an existing MFA method' and 'For a new MFA method' on Page 4.
concerns.output_quote: {"text": "For an existing MFA method"}, {"text": "For a new MFA method"} inside a 'list' element.

concerns.analysis: On page 4, the entire procedural instruction text (steps 1-5 for both the existing and new MFA methods) is missing from the converted output. The reading order jumps from the converted sub-headers directly to image crops, omitting ten major steps of text content found in the source. This is a material divergence from the source's intended information flow.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Steps 1-5 for 'For an existing MFA method' and Steps 1-5 for 'For a new MFA method' on Page 4.
concerns.output_quote: [Content entirely absent from JSON output after the list at reading_order 60]

concerns.analysis: Several meaningful UI icons and graphics present in the source PDF are missing from the HTML output or have been replaced by text placeholders. On page 3, the 'edit' icon is missing and replaced with text. On page 4, the radio button ('Set as default') and checkbox ('Make as default') graphics are completely missing. On page 5, the 'trash' icon is missing and replaced with text. These are meaningful instructional images referred to in the text steps.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Source PDF Page 3 shows pencil 'edit' icons. Page 4 shows a radio button graphic for 'Set as default' and a checkbox graphic for 'Make as default'. Page 5 shows a 'trash' icon.
concerns.output_quote: HTML output replaces edit icons with '[edit icon]' text, replaces trash icon with '[trash icon]' text, and completely omits the radio button and checkbox graphics under 'Change default MFA enrollment method'.
concerns.location: Modify MFA enrollment (Page 3), Change default MFA enrollment method (Page 4), Delete MFA method (Page 5)

concerns.analysis: The procedural steps throughout the document are presented as numbered (ordered) lists in the source PDF. The output converts all of these into 'unordered' lists, losing the sequential meaning. Additionally, on page 4, the structural layout of headings and subsequent steps is collapsed into a simple two-item unordered list containing only the sub-headers.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Numbered procedural steps (e.g., 1. Login to..., 2. Click on..., etc.) throughout the document.
concerns.location: Throughout the document (e.g., sections[0], sections[2], sections[4])

concerns.analysis: The URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' is clearly visible and clickable in the source PDF on every page. In the output, for pages 1 and 2 (sections[0] and sections[1]), the URL is entirely missing from the text strings (e.g., 'Login to '). While the text is present on later pages, the conversion fails to create a functional link object for these government URLs.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Step 1 on Page 1: '1. Login to https://myncid.nc.gov/'

action_items: Restore the 10 missing procedural steps and instructional text on Page 4.
Re-integrate omitted URLs and button labels in the 'Email', 'Text Message', and 'Phone' sections.
Repair the heading hierarchy on Page 4 and restore the sequential list formatting.
Ensure all meaningful instructional graphics and icons are present and have appropriate alternative text.
